---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code. 

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Ctrl+Shift+Enter*. 

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(caret)
library(e1071)
library(pROC)
library(MLmetrics) # For F1_Score and AUC

#read data
# LOAD AND PREP DATA
cho <- read.table("cho.txt", header=FALSE)

# Rename and Clean
colnames(cho)[1:2] <- c("gene_id", "true_label")

# We ONLY want the label and the features for the SVM
# Column 2 is our target, Column 3 onwards are features
cho_data <- cho[, -1] # Remove gene_id
colnames(cho_data)[1] <- "Class"

# CRITICAL: Convert to factor and make names valid (C1, C2...)
cho_data$Class <- as.factor(cho_data$Class)
levels(cho_data$Class) <- paste0("Class", levels(cho_data$Class))

# RUN THE LOOP (three fold, t=3)
set.seed(123)
cho_results <- list(Acc = c(), F1 = c(), AUC = c())

for(i in 1:3) {
  # Split 80/20
  trainIndex <- createDataPartition(cho_data$Class, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)
  cho_train <- cho_data[trainIndex, ]
  cho_test  <- cho_data[-trainIndex, ]
  
  # 3-fold CV Control
  control_cho <- trainControl(method="cv", 
                              number=3, 
                              classProbs=TRUE, 
                              summaryFunction=multiClassSummary)
  
  # Train SVM with PCA Preprocessing
  # This uses the 'Class ~ .' notation which means "predict Class using all other columns"
  svm_cho <- train(Class ~ ., 
                   data = cho_train, 
                   method = "svmRadial",
                   preProcess = c("center", "scale", "pca"),
                   trControl = control_cho,
                   tuneLength = 5)
  
  # Evaluate using the helper function we built earlier
  res <- evaluate_model(svm_cho, cho_test, cho_test$Class)
  
  cho_results$Acc[i] <- res$Accuracy
  cho_results$F1[i]  <- res$F1
  cho_results$AUC[i] <- res$AUC
}

# 3. FINAL OUTPUT
cat("\n--- FINAL CHO RESULTS (Mean ± SD) ---\n")
cat(sprintf("Accuracy: %.4f ± %.4f\n", mean(cho_results$Acc), sd(cho_results$Acc)))
cat(sprintf("F1 Score: %.4f ± %.4f\n", mean(cho_results$F1), sd(cho_results$F1)))
cat(sprintf("AUC:      %.4f ± %.4f\n", mean(cho_results$AUC), sd(cho_results$AUC)))

```

```{r}
#install.packages("dslabs")
# 1. LOAD DATA
# If you don't have dslabs: install.packages("dslabs")
library(dslabs)
mnist <- read_mnist()

# The pixels are in 'image' and labels in 'labels'
x_train <- mnist$train$images / 255  # Normalize pixels to [0,1]
y_train <- as.factor(mnist$train$labels)
x_test  <- mnist$test$images / 255
y_test  <- as.factor(mnist$test$labels)

# 2. RENAME CLASSES FOR CARET
# R needs names like "Digit0" instead of just "0" for probability math
levels(y_train) <- paste0("Digit", levels(y_train))
levels(y_test)  <- paste0("Digit", levels(y_test))

# 3. DIMENSIONALITY REDUCTION (PCA)
# PCA is essential here. SVM on 784 features is too slow.
pca_mnist <- prcomp(x_train, rank. = 50) # Reduce to top 50 components
train_pca <- as.data.frame(pca_mnist$x)
train_pca$Class <- y_train

# 4. SUBSET 10% FOR TUNING (As per assignment instructions)
set.seed(123)
valIndex <- createDataPartition(train_pca$Class, p = 0.1, list = FALSE)
mnist_sub_train <- train_pca[valIndex, ] # This is your ~6,000 sample training set

# 5. TRAIN SVM
# We use the e1071 'svm' directly for large datasets as it's more memory efficient
library(e1071)
svm_mnist <- svm(Class ~ ., 
                 data = mnist_sub_train, 
                 kernel = "radial", 
                 cost = 10, 
                 gamma = 0.01, 
                 probability = TRUE)

# 6. EVAL ON FULL TEST SET
evaluate_model <- function(model, test_data, test_labels) {
  # 1. Get Class Predictions
  preds <- predict(model, test_data)
  
  # 2. Get Probability Predictions (Specific to e1071 SVM)
  pred_probs <- predict(model, test_data, probability = TRUE)
  probs <- attr(pred_probs, "probabilities")
  
  # 3. Calculate Accuracy
  acc <- mean(preds == test_labels)
  
  # 4. Calculate F1 Score (using MLmetrics)
  # We use 'macro' or 'micro' averaging for multi-class
  f1 <- F1_Score(y_pred = preds, y_true = test_labels)
  
  # 5. Calculate Multi-class AUC
  # multiclass.roc needs the labels and the matrix of probabilities
  roc_obj <- multiclass.roc(test_labels, probs)
  auc_val <- as.numeric(auc(roc_obj))
  
  return(list(Accuracy = acc, F1 = f1, AUC = auc_val))
}

test_pca <- as.data.frame(predict(pca_mnist, x_test))
mnist_results <- evaluate_model(svm_mnist, test_pca, y_test)

# 7. DISPLAY RESULTS
cat("\n--- MNIST SVM Results (10% subset + PCA) ---\n")
print(as.data.frame(mnist_results))
```
